Graph break: Termination of tracing when Dynamo encounters unsupported control flow, Python syntax, or third-party library calls. Dynamo saves the current FX graph, restores local context, hands control back to the Python interpreter, then resumes tracing to build the next graph.
Symbolic execution: Step-by-step symbolic bytecode execution of a Python program, where Dynamo wraps tensors as proxies, propagates them through each opcode, and records tensor computations as nodes to build the FX graph.
Guards: Pytorch runtime predicts attached to a compiled graph that validates inputs, object identities, types, shapes, strides, control-flow–relevant values, and other observable program states.
Side-effect: Modeling, tracking, and preserving observable state changes (e.g., mutations, aliasing, RNG/global state, I/O) so compiled execution matches eager semantics.
